 title: Inline Toast
 description: An inline copy toast component with animated feedback when copying code to clipboard.
 ---
 
 import { InlineToastPreview } from "@/registry/new-york/inline-toast-demo";
 
 <ComponentPreview name="inline-toast">
   <InlineToastPreview />
 </ComponentPreview>
 
 ## Installation
 
 If you've already added the prsui registry, use the short command:
 
 <CodeBlockCommand
   __bun__="bunx --bun shadcn@latest add @prsui/inline-toast"
   __npm__="npx shadcn@latest add @prsui/inline-toast"
   __pnpm__="pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @prsui/inline-toast"
   __yarn__="yarn shadcn@latest add @prsui/inline-toast"
 />
 
 Otherwise, use the full registry URL:
 
 <CodeBlockCommand
   __bun__="bunx --bun shadcn@latest add https://prsui.pr5.dev/r/inline-toast.json"
   __npm__="npx shadcn@latest add https://prsui.pr5.dev/r/inline-toast.json"
   __pnpm__="pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add https://prsui.pr5.dev/r/inline-toast.json"
   __yarn__="yarn shadcn@latest add https://prsui.pr5.dev/r/inline-toast.json"
 />
 
 ## Usage
 
 After you add the component with the CLI, import it from your UI folder:
 
 ```tsx
 import { InlineToast } from "@/components/ui/inline-toast";
 
 export function MyComponent() {
-  return <InlineToast code="CPT8456Y9" copyDuration={2000}/>;
+  return <InlineToast code="CPT8456Y9" copyDuration={2000} />;
 }
 ```
 
 ## Props
 
 | Prop           | Type     | Default | Description                                    |
 | -------------- | -------- | ------- | ---------------------------------------------- |
 | `code`         | `string` | -       | The code string to display and copy            |
 | `copyDuration` | `number` | `2000`  | Duration in ms to show the copied confirmation |

+++ content/docs/components/view-on-map.mdx	
@@ -0,0 +1,56 @@
+---
+title: View On Map
+description: An animated map preview component that expands from a pill button into an embedded Google Map view.
+---
+
+import { ViewOnMapPreview } from "@/registry/new-york/view-on-map-demo";
+
+<ComponentPreview name="view-on-map">
+  <ViewOnMapPreview />
+</ComponentPreview>
+
+## Installation
+
+If you've already added the prsui registry, use the short command:
+
+<CodeBlockCommand
+  __bun__="bunx --bun shadcn@latest add @prsui/view-on-map"
+  __npm__="npx shadcn@latest add @prsui/view-on-map"
+  __pnpm__="pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @prsui/view-on-map"
+  __yarn__="yarn shadcn@latest add @prsui/view-on-map"
+/>
+
+Otherwise, use the full registry URL:
+
+<CodeBlockCommand
+  __bun__="bunx --bun shadcn@latest add https://prsui.pr5.dev/r/view-on-map.json"
+  __npm__="npx shadcn@latest add https://prsui.pr5.dev/r/view-on-map.json"
+  __pnpm__="pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add https://prsui.pr5.dev/r/view-on-map.json"
+  __yarn__="yarn shadcn@latest add https://prsui.pr5.dev/r/view-on-map.json"
+/>
+
+## Usage
+
+After you add the component with the CLI, import it from your UI folder:
+
+```tsx
+import { ViewOnMap } from "@/components/ui/view-on-map";
+
+export function MyComponent() {
+  return (
+    <ViewOnMap
+      address="Boston Public Garden"
+      mapImageUrl="https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1526778548025-fa2f459cd5ce?q=80&w=2000&auto=format&fit=crop"
+    />
+  );
+}
+```
+
+## Props
+
+| Prop           | Type     | Default | Description                                      |
+| -------------- | -------- | ------- | ------------------------------------------------ |
+| `locationName` | `string` | -       | Optional location name (reserved for future use) |
+| `address`      | `string` | -       | The address to display on the map                |
+| `mapImageUrl`  | `string` | -       | Background image URL for the pill button         |
+| `className`    | `string` | `""`    | Additional CSS classes                           |

---
title: Editable Chip
description: An inline-editable chip component with animated transitions between display and edit modes.
---

import { EditableChipPreview } from "@/registry/new-york/editable-chip-demo";

<ComponentPreview name="editable-chip">
  <EditableChipPreview />
</ComponentPreview>

## Installation

If you've already added the prsui registry, use the short command:

<CodeBlockCommand
  __bun__="bunx --bun shadcn@latest add @prsui/editable-chip"
  __npm__="npx shadcn@latest add @prsui/editable-chip"
  __pnpm__="pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @prsui/editable-chip"
  __yarn__="yarn shadcn@latest add @prsui/editable-chip"
/>

Otherwise, use the full registry URL:

<CodeBlockCommand
  __bun__="bunx --bun shadcn@latest add https://prsui.pr5.dev/r/editable-chip.json"
  __npm__="npx shadcn@latest add https://prsui.pr5.dev/r/editable-chip.json"
  __pnpm__="pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add https://prsui.pr5.dev/r/editable-chip.json"
  __yarn__="yarn shadcn@latest add https://prsui.pr5.dev/r/editable-chip.json"
/>

## Usage

After you add the component with the CLI, import it from your UI folder:

```tsx
import { EditableChip } from "@/components/ui/editable-chip";

export function MyComponent() {
  return (
    <EditableChip
      defaultLabel="Watchlist"
      onChange={(value) => console.log(value)}
    />
  );
}
```

## Props

| Prop              | Type                      | Default       | Description                               |
| ----------------- | ------------------------- | ------------- | ----------------------------------------- |
| `defaultLabel`    | `string`                  | `"Watchlist"` | Initial text displayed in the chip        |
| `showThemeToggle` | `boolean`                 | -             | Whether to show a theme toggle (reserved) |
| `onChange`        | `(value: string) => void` | -             | Callback fired when the label is saved    |

import { ViewOnMap } from "@/components/ui/view-on-map";
